what_it_does: Declares a stack item type named 'presentation' for an operator-facing item that is shown to the user and cleared by acknowledgement; it defines the type's id, label, description, a single row field mapping 'headline' to 'identity.headline', an unsettled state of 'pending', and comments describing a generic fallback resolve/spotlight behavior until a presentation surface exists.
observations: The docstring frames the type as a PRESENTATION stack for the operator, cleared by ACKNOWLEDGE.
The feed and host renderer are intentionally declared now but implemented later, so the operator-cycle vocabulary is complete.
The host is expected to soft-degrade/fail-loud by showing the name and flagging it '--unready' until a renderer lands.
STACK_ITEM_TYPE is a module-level dictionary with id 'presentation', label 'Presentation', a desc string, row_fields, and unsettled_state 'pending'.
The row field 'headline' is bound to the string 'identity.headline' as a placeholder for the fork's stack-feed shape.
The 'open_verb' entry is only a comment, not an actual key/value pair.
standouts: Declares a UI-facing concept as a ledger stack item type before its renderer exists.
Explicit degradation policy: show name + '--unready', never blank, never lost.
Uses 'identity.headline' as a deferred field reference for the eventual feed shape.
conventions: Docstring doubles as design rationale and future-behavior contract.
String-based field references ('identity.headline') for row fields.
concerns: The 'open_verb' key is commented out and not actually defined.
'identity.headline' is referenced but not defined or explained in this file.
The host renderer and acknowledge clearing behavior are described in comments but not implemented here.
notes: This file registers the 'presentation' stack item type and documents its intended lifecycle and placeholder rendering policy before the actual feed or renderer is built.
questions: What is the exact shape of the fork's stack-feed that will finalize the headline field?
What value or structure will 'open_verb' take when implemented?
How is the '--unready' flag rendered and what triggers acknowledgement?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose is to declare the type now so the vocabulary is complete and the host can degrade honestly; the file does exactly that, using placeholders and comments for the not-yet-landed parts.
apparent_themes: Completeness of operator-cycle vocabulary before implementation.
Separation of type declaration from renderer implementation.
intention_signals: It appears to be trying to establish a first-class operator-facing 'presentation' item in the stack, ensure the system can show something meaningful even when the renderer is missing, and define acknowledgement as the resolution action.
novelty: It treats a presentation surface as a ledger-declared stack item type with an explicit unsettled/unready state and a deferred renderer binding, rather than leaving UI elements implicit.
contribution: Provides a vocabulary entry and lifecycle contract for operator-facing presentation items, including a placeholder headline field and a policy for honest degradation when the renderer is absent.
summary_for_embedding: A ledger declaration of the 'presentation' stack item type, describing an operator-facing item cleared by acknowledgement with a placeholder headline field and an unsettled pending state.
intention_for_embedding: To complete the operator-cycle vocabulary early and define how the system should degrade when a presentation renderer is not yet available.
